original_abstract:
This research is driven by the growing concern that many students lack a foundational understanding of religious teachings derived from their original sources. This deficiency often leads to frequent disagreements, the spread of misinformation, and a tendency to defend personal biases without critical clarification. Such issues can be mitigated by enhancing students' religious literacy and deepening their comprehension of theological principles. 

This study aims to evaluate the conceptualization and implementation of the *Agama Keagamaan dan Pendalaman Kitab-Kitab* (AKPK) program at SMPN 1 Darangdan, identify the factors that support or hinder its execution, and assess the religious values students acquire through the program’s curriculum. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive methodology, data were gathered through observation, interviews, and documentation, and subsequently analyzed through data reduction, display, and conclusion drawing. 

The findings reveal that: (1) The AKPK program at SMPN 1 Darangdan is systematically managed through the POAC framework (Planning, Organizing, Actuating, and Controlling) to meet educational objectives. (2) Key supporting factors include the availability of digital materials and guidebooks, a dedicated managerial team, and active teacher involvement. Conversely, a significant hurdle is the number of students experiencing Arabic illiteracy; this has been addressed by increasing instructional hours for the program. (3) The religious values students gained include a strengthened relationship with the Divine (*illahiyyah*) and improved social conduct (*insaniyyah*), alongside a deeper appreciation for the role of worship in daily life.
humanized_abstract:
A reason for this research is that the large number of students do not possess enough knowledge of religious teachings or the source of the religions. They get engaged into many disputes, misconceptions and do not admit any argument to clarify their incorrect views. So, they need to learn religion to become literate and aware in terms of religious concepts.

This study aims to describe, explain and analyze the implementation of AKPK (Agama Keagamaan dan Pendalaman Kitab-Kitab) at SMPN 1 Darangdan, the factors influencing its implementation and the religious values learned by students through the curriculum. The research was conducted descriptively using qualitative method. The data were collected by observing the learning process of students at SMPN 1 Darangdan, through interviews with educators and students and by collecting data from relevant documents. The data analysis was processed by reducing, displaying and concluding.

Berdasarkan hasil penelitian tersebut, ada beberapa penjelasan sebagai berikut: (1) Pelaksanaan program AKPK di SMPN 1 Darangdan dipandu oleh model POAC yaitu Planning, Organizing, Actuating, dan Controlling untuk mencapai mencapa tertujuya tujuan pendidikan. (2) Perancang model POAC terdiri dari berbagai alat pengajar yang bersifat edukatif yaitu bahan karya tulis digital yang sesuai dengan kerangka pokok pengantar agama Islam kewiraan murid (AKPK) yang dilengkapi dengan guidebook yang dipahami dan dirasakan langsung oleh seluruh pengawas yang berwenang dan guru–guru. (3) Kemudian model POAC tersebut dipandu oleh manajemen yang menampung, menyalurkan dan mengendalikan tujuan dan pencapaian tujuan program AKPK; kemudian dimana belajar mengulang-ulang apa saja pokok materi yang dapat dibangun ke kemudian oleh warga sekolah atau di suluh dengan kemudian kembali ke khalayak masyarakat; kemudian mantera mantera yaitu, dengan meningkatkan jam pembelajaran yang dimana peserta didik diperlunakan kehadiran Arabic illiteracy.

document.input:
This research is driven by the growing concern that many students lack a foundational understanding of religious teachings derived from their original sources. This deficiency often leads to frequent disagreements, the spread of misinformation, and a tendency to defend personal biases without critical clarification. Such issues can be mitigated by enhancing students' religious literacy and deepening their comprehension of theological principles. 

This study aims to evaluate the conceptualization and implementation of the *Agama Keagamaan dan Pendalaman Kitab-Kitab* (AKPK) program at SMPN 1 Darangdan, identify the factors that support or hinder its execution, and assess the religious values students acquire through the program’s curriculum. The findings reveal that: (1) The AKPK program at SMPN 1 Darangdan is systematically managed through the POAC framework (Planning, Organizing, Actuating, and Controlling) to meet educational objectives. (2) Key supporting factors include the availability of digital materials and guidebooks, a dedicated managerial team, and active teacher involvement. Conversely, a significant hurdle is the number of students experiencing Arabic illiteracy; this has been addressed by increasing instructional hours for the program. (3) The religious values students gained include a strengthened relationship with the Divine (*illahiyyah*) and improved social conduct (*insaniyyah*), alongside a deeper appreciation for the role of worship in daily life.
